metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3718
metadata.comment:
Rationale: A peptide derived from the SNARE domain of syntaxin 3B inhibits functional refilling in bipolar neurons. The amplitude of the exocytotic response evoked by the first depolarization was not significantly different between cells dialysed with the syntaxin 3B SNARE peptide (ΔCm=138±27.4 fF, n=5) and the scrambled peptide (ΔCm=162±22.2 fF, n=5). Upon subsequent stimulation rundown in Six-3b peptide dialysed terminals declined more than scrambled peptide.

19/6/2018 Pim
- From abstract: "Synaptic vesicle exocytosis in conventional synapses is mediated by the interaction between the synaptic vesicle protein synaptobrevin2/VAMP2 and the plasma membrane proteins syntaxin 1 and SNAP-25 (reviewed in: Rizo and Rosenmund, 2008; Südhof and Rothman, 2009). These vesicular and plasma membrane proteins interact via the so called SNARE (soluble N-ethylmaleimide-sensitive factor attachment protein receptor) domains and are thought to catalyze synaptic vesicle exocytosis. Ribbon synapses in the mammalian retina contain synaptobrevin2/VAMP2 and SNAP-25, but lack syntaxin 1. Instead, they contain the related isoform syntaxin 3B (Curtis et al., 2008; Sherry et al., 2006; Morgans et al. 1996,).". Experimental description: M&M: Goldfish (Carassius auratus) bipolar neurons
